extra.transcript:
The Mermaid tool's primary artifact — a self-contained **SVG** — has been produced for tldraw/tldraw.

**Files saved in the working directory** (`/private/var/folders/18/r43jk_cx473628dkrjj_7ylh0000gn/T/ax-docs-jqZN9l/`):

- **`tldraw.svg`** ← primary artifact (130 KB, well-formed vector, renders inline in any browser)
- `tldraw.mmd` — the diffable Mermaid DSL source
- `puppeteer.json` — config pointing `mmdc` at the local Chrome (cache was empty)

**What the diagram represents** (from `PROJECT.md`), structured as a left-to-right flowchart with color-coded groups:

| Layer | Nodes | Role |
|---|---|---|
| **Store** (reactive records) | `shapes`, `pages`, `camera`, `assets`, `history` | Reactive data records; undo/redo history |
| **Editor** (API surface) | `api`, `select`, `events` | Public API, selection/tooling, signals/side effects |
| **Shape utils** (per-shape) | `geo`, `render`, `props` | Geometry/hit-testing, React render component, props validation |
| **React canvas** (view) | `canvas`, `overlays`, `ui` | DOM container, overlays/hints, toolbar |
| **Serialization** | `snapshot`, `.tldr` JSON, `load/migrate` | Snapshots ↔ `.tldr` JSON + load/migrate |

Key flows shown: user input → Editor → reads/writes Store records; Store feeds shape geometry & render → React canvas; Store ↔ external sync; snapshots serialize to `.tldr` JSON and re-load/migrate back into the Store. Dashed edge marks the reactive-signal relationship between Store and Editor.

Note: the bundled `chrome-headless-shell` cache was empty, so rendering used a `puppeteer.json` pointing `mmdc` at the installed Google Chrome on this machine — SVG output verified as well-formed XML.

The primary artifact for the **mermaid** tool is an **SVG diagram** (self-contained vector, renders inline with no viewer). I rendered the tldraw/tldraw architecture diagram from its Mermaid DSL source and saved the validated SVG to the working directory.

## Result

**Saved to working directory:**
- `tldraw.mmd` — Mermaid DSL source (diffable plain text)
- `tldraw.svg` — rendered vector artifact (130 KB, validated via `@mermaid-js/mermaid-cli`)

## What the diagram represents

The `.mmd` models the tldraw/tldraw architecture from `PROJECT.md` as a left-to-right flowchart with five subgraphs plus external actors:

| Layer | Contents | Role |
|---|---|---|
| **Store** (reactive records) | `shapes[]`, `pages[]`, `camera`, `assets[]`, `history / undo-redo` | Source of truth |
| **Editor** (API surface) | Public API, Selection/tools, Signals & side effects | The API consumers code against |
| **ShapeUtils** (per-shape) | Geometry/hit-testing, React render component, Props/validation | Per-shape geometry + render |
| **View** (React canvas) | Canvas/DOM container, Overlays & hints, UI/toolbar | What the user sees |
| **Serialization** | Snapshot → `.tldr JSON` → Load/migrate | Persistence round-trip |
| External | `User input`, `External store / sync` | Drivers |

Key data-flow edges captured:
- `User → Editor` (input drives the API)
- `Editor ⇄ Store` (read/write records)
- `Store -.reactive signals.-> Editor` (reactivity)
- `shapes → geometry/render → canvas` (store drives shape utils drive the canvas)
- `Store → Snapshot → .tldr → Load → Store` (serialization round-trip)
- `External ⇄ Store` (sync)

Color classes distinguish record (blue), API (purple), shape-utils (green), and view (orange) tiers, matching the conceptual layers in `PROJECT.md`.

## Validation notes
- Renderer: `npx -y @mermaid-js/mermaid-cli` (headless Chromium) with `puppeteer.json` pointing at the installed Chrome.
- Output verified: 35 `<rect>` nodes, 84 `foreignObject` labels, 42 label spans, title `tldraw/tldraw — Architecture`, well-formed single-root `<svg>` with viewBox. The artifact opens directly in any browser and is the canonical deliverable for this toolchain.
